microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.Description: Predictive autoscale utilizes machine learning to efficiently manage and scale Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets by forecasting CPU load through historical usage analysis, ensuring timely scale-out to meet demand.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets' | where iff(isempty(tostring(tags['aks-managed-poolName'])), true, false) | project name, id=tolower(id), tags | join kind=leftouter ( resources | where type == 'microsoft.insights/autoscalesettings' | where tostring(properties.targetResourceUri) contains 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachineScaleSets' | project id = tolower(tostring(properties.targetResourceUri)), autoscalesettings = properties ) on id | where autoscalesettings.enabled == 'true' and autoscalesettings.predictiveAutoscalePolicy.scaleMode == 'Disabled' | project recommendationId = '3f85a51c-e286-9f44-b4dc-51d00768696c', name, id, tags, param1 = 'predictiveAutoscalePolicy_scaleMode: Disabled' | order by id asc

microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.Description: Windows Server 2022 retires when Kubernetes 1.34 reaches the end of platform support. On Kubernetes 1.35 and later, creation of new Windows Server 2022 node pools is blocked.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets' and tags has 'aks-managed' and tostring(properties.virtualMachineProfile.storageProfile.imageReference.id) contains 'AKSWindows/images/windows-2022'|project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.Description: Enabling automatic instance repairs helps achieve high availability by maintaining a set of healthy instances. If an unhealthy instance is found by the Application Health extension or load balancer health probe, automatic instance repairs attempt to recover the instance by triggering repair actions.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets" | where isnotnull(properties.virtualMachineProfile) | where isnull(properties.automaticRepairsPolicy) or properties.automaticRepairsPolicy.enabled == false | project subscriptionId,id

microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.Description: Windows Server 2019 retires when Kubernetes 1.32 reaches the end of platform support. On Kubernetes 1.33 and later, creation of new Windows Server 2019 node pools is blocked.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets' and tags has 'aks-managed' and tostring(properties.virtualMachineProfile.storageProfile.imageReference.id) contains 'AKSWindows/images/windows-2019'|project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.Description: Configuring VMSS application health monitoring using the Application Health extension or load balancer health probes enables the Azure platform to improve the resiliency of your application by responding to changes in application health.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescaleset.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets" | where isnull(properties.virtualMachineProfile.networkProfile.healthProbe.id) | join kind=leftouter ( resources | where type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets" | mv-expand extension=properties.virtualMachineProfile.extensionProfile.extensions | where extension.properties.type in ( "ApplicationHealthWindows", "ApplicationHealthLinux" ) | project id ) on id | where id1 == "" | project subscriptionId,id

microsoft.eventgrid/domains.Description: Support for TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 ends on the retirement date.
microsoft.eventgrid/domains.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.eventgrid/domains' and tostring(properties['minimumTlsVersionAllowed']) in ('1.1','1.0') | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers.Description: Deploying Azure Load Balancer backend pools with at least two instances prevents a single point of failure. Pairing with Virtual Machine Scale Sets can provide additional scalability.
Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers' | mv-expand bpool = properties.backendAddressPools | extend BackendAddresses = array_length(bpool.properties.loadBalancerBackendAddresses) | where BackendAddresses <= 1 | project id, subscriptionId

Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers.Description: To avoid service disruptions, migrate to Inbound NAT rule V2.
Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/loadbalancers' and sku.name =~ 'Standard' | extend inboundNATRULES=todynamic(properties['inboundNatRules']) | mv-expand inboundNATRULES | where isnotempty(inboundNATRULES.properties.backendIPConfiguration) and array_length(properties.inboundNatPools) == 0 | project id, subscriptionId

Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers.Description: To keep the workloads appropriately distributed, you need to upgrade to Standard Load Balancer.
Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers' | where sku.name == 'Basic' | project id, subscriptionId

Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers.Description: Outbound rules for Standard Public Load Balancer involve manual port allocation for backend pools, limiting scalability and risk of SNAT port exhaustion. NAT Gateway is recommended for its dynamic scaling and secure internet connectivity.
Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers' | extend outboundRules = array_length(properties.outboundRules) | where outboundRules > 0 | project recommendationId = '8d319a05-677b-944f-b9b4-ca0fb42e883c', name, id, tags, Param1 = 'outboundRules: >=1'

Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses.Description: If you have any Basic SKU public IP addresses deployed in Azure Cloud Services (extended support), those deployments aren't affected by this retirement and you don't need to take any action for them.

microsoft.labservices/labplans.Description: After the retirement date, Azure Lab Services aren't supported. You will not have access to the lab accounts, lab plans, or labs.

Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances.Description: Configuring zone redundancy option for backups copies your backup file synchronously across three Azure availability zones in the primary region. If Geo is selected, then it copies your data asynchronously three times to a single physical location in the paired secondary region.
Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances' | extend backupredundancy=properties.storageAccountType | extend ServiceTier = sku.tier | where backupredundancy != 'GeoZone' | project recommendationId='15e2712c-f3ea-4a8d-9081-11e822b1ccfb', name, id, param1=strcat('Service Tier:', ServiceTier), param2=strcat('Backup Redundancy:', backupredundancy)

Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances.Description: Redirect mode enables direct connectivity to the instance bypassing the local gateway component and resulting in improved latency and throughput. Redirect mode applies to the VNet-local endpoint only, while the public and private endpoint will always operate in Proxy connection mode.
Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances' | extend connectionpolicy=properties.proxyOverride | where connectionpolicy != 'Redirect' | project recommendationId='c14de326-2729-4be7-a91f-4ea185d24b10', name, id, tags, param1=strcat('Connection Policy:', connectionpolicy)

Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances.Description: Upgrade TLS to latest version. Support for TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 in Azure SQL Managed Instance is ending.
Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances.ARG_Query: resources|where type =~ 'microsoft.sql/managedinstances' and properties['minimalTlsVersion'] in ('None', '1.0','1.1')|project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts.Description: You can no longer use AI Services Personalizer with the applications after the retirement date.

microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts.Description: Beginning October 1, 2022, you aren't be able to create new QnA Maker resources or knowledge bases. All QnA Maker resources and knowledge bases created before that date are supported until the retirement date.
microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts' and kind == 'QnAMaker' | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts.Description: Until the retirement date, continue use of AI Services Anomaly Detector resources.
microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts' and kind == 'AnomalyDetector' | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Databricks/workspaces.Description: Standard tier workspaces are retiring. Upgrade to Premium tier prior to retirement date. The Standard tier workspace is automatically upgraded to Premium tier after the retirement date.
Microsoft.Databricks/workspaces.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Databricks/workspaces' and sku =~ '{"name":"standard"}' | project id,subscriptionId

microsoft.cdn/profiles.Description: Azure CDN Standard from Microsoft (classic) is scheduled for retirement on 30 September 2027. We encourage you to use the zero downtime migration tool to transition to Front Door Standard and Premium SKUs. These options offer not only feature parity but also additional features and enhanced security
microsoft.cdn/profiles.ARG_Query: resources | where type == "microsoft.cdn/profiles" and kind == "cdn" | where sku contains "Standard_Microsoft" | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.cdn/profiles.Description: To avoid service disruptions, you need to migrate to Azure Front Door Standard or Premium
microsoft.cdn/profiles.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.cdn/profiles' and sku contains 'Standard_Microsoft' and kind == 'cdn' | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.network/expressrouteports.Description: Provisioning ExpressRoute circuits on a 10-Gbps or 100-Gbps ExpressRoute Direct resource up to 20-Gbps or 200-Gbps is possible but not recommended for resiliency. If an ExpressRoute Direct port fails, and circuits are using full capacity, the remaining port won't handle the extra load.
microsoft.network/expressrouteports.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.network/expressrouteports' | where toint(properties['provisionedBandwidthInGbps']) > toint(properties['bandwidthInGbps']) | project recommendationId = '0bee356b-7348-4799-8cab-0c71ffe13018', name, id, tags, param1 = strcat('provisionedBandwidthInGbps: ', properties['provisionedBandwidthInGbps']), param2 = strcat('bandwidthInGbps: ', properties['bandwidthInGbps'])

microsoft.network/expressrouteports.Description: In Azure ExpressRoute Direct, the Admin State specifies the administrative status of the layer 1 link as Enabled or Disabled. The Admin State is the same as turning the physical port on or off. Billing begins when Admin State for either link is set to Enabled.
microsoft.network/expressrouteports.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.network/expressrouteports' | where properties['links'][0]['properties']['adminState'] == 'Disabled' or properties['links'][1]['properties']['adminState'] == 'Disabled' | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.Description: After the retirement date, workloads running on kubenet networking for AKS aren't supported.
microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | where properties.networkProfile.networkPlugin == 'kubenet' | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.Description: Azure CNI improves cluster networking with dynamic IP allocation, scalable subnets, and direct pod-to-VNET connectivity. It supports Azure Network Policies and Calico for pods and nodes, optimizing efficiency and security
microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | extend networkProfile = tostring (parse_json(properties.networkProfile.networkPlugin)) | where networkProfile =='kubenet' | project recommendationId='c22db132-399b-4e7c-995d-577a60881be8', name, id, tags, param1=strcat('networkProfile :',networkProfile)

microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.Description: Azure Network Policy Manager for Azure Kubernetes Service clusters running Linux nodes is retiring. Migrate to Cilium Network Policy using Azure Container Networking Interface powered by Cilium before the retirement date.
microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | where tolower(tostring(properties.networkProfile.networkPolicy)) =~ 'azure' | extend agentPools = properties.agentPoolProfiles | where array_length(agentPools) > 0 | mv-expand agentPool = properties.agentPoolProfiles | where agentPool.osType !~ 'Windows' | distinct id, subscriptionId

microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.Description: Migrate to Container insights managed identity authentication before the retirement date to maintain access and retain functionality.
microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters'| extend aksproperties = parse_json(tolower(properties)) | extend isEnabled = aksproperties.addonprofiles.omsagent.enabled | extend useAADAuth = aksproperties.addonprofiles.omsagent.config.useaadauth | where isEnabled =~ 'true' and useAADAuth != 'true' | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.Description: From Kubernetes 1.26, Azure Disk and Azure File in-tree drivers are deprecated in favor of CSI drivers. Existing deployments remain operational but untested; users should switch to CSI drivers for new features and SKUs.
microsoft.containerservice/managedcluste.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters' | where properties.storageProfile.diskCSIDriver.enabled != true or properties.storageProfile.fileCSIDriver.enabled != true or properties.storageProfile.blobCSIDriver.enabled != true | project recommendationId = 'b002c030-72e6-4a37-8217-1cb276c43169', name, id, tags

microsoft.maps/accounts.Description: Follow our instructions to use Gen2 Price Tier for new Azure Maps accounts being created via Azure Resource Manager templates to avoid deployment disruptions
microsoft.maps/accounts.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.maps/accounts' and (sku has 'S1' or sku has 'S0') | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters.Description: Migrate to Azure Kubernetes Service on Azure Local for enhanced capabilities. The platform recommends migrating to Azure Kubernetes Service on Azure Local for improved features.
microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters' | where properties['distribution'] in ('aks_management','aks_workload') | where properties['infrastructure'] contains 'vSphere' | where properties['kind'] !~ ('ProvisionedCluster') | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters.Description: Deploying, upgrading, and scaling Azure Kubernetes Service on Windows Server 2019 and Windows Server 2022 is blocked.
microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters' | where properties['distribution'] in ('aks_management','aks_workload') | where properties['infrastructure'] == 'azure_stack_hci' | where ['kind'] != ('ProvisionedCluster') | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters.Description: Azure Kubernetes Service's current architecture on Windows Server 2019 and Windows Server 2022 is retiring. Replace existing Azure Kubernetes Service clusters on Windows Server 2019, Windows Server 2022 or Azure Local, version 22H2 with Azure Kubernetes Service on Azure Local, version 23H2 or later.
microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.kubernetes/connectedclusters' and properties['distribution'] in~ ('aks_management','aks_workload') and properties['infrastructure'] =~ 'azure_stack_hci' and ['kind'] != ('ProvisionedCluster') | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.cache/redis.ARG_Query: resources | where subscriptionId == 'fa5349cd-ab3c-4232-88e5-8b842489a230' | where type == 'microsoft.cache/redis' | take 50 | project id, subscriptionId, name

microsoft.cache/redis.Description: Geo-Replication enables disaster recovery for cached data, even in the unlikely event of a widespread regional failure. This can be essential for mission-critical applications. We recommend that you configure passive geo-replication for Premium Azure Cache for Redis instances.
microsoft.cache/redis.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ "microsoft.cache/redis" and properties['sku']['name'] =~ "premium" and array_length(properties['linkedServers']) == 0 and properties['redisConfiguration']['rdb-backup-enabled'] != "true" and properties['redisConfiguration']['aof-backup-enabled'] != "true" and isempty(properties['redisConfiguration']['zonal-configuration']) and isnull(properties['replicasPerPrimary']) and isnull(properties['replicasPerMaster']) | project id, subscriptionId
resources | where subscriptionId == 'fa5349cd-ab3c-4232-88e5-8b842489a230' | where type == 'microsoft.cache/redis' | take 50 | project id, subscriptionId, name

microsoft.cache/redis.Description: To avoid service disruptions, migrate workloads to Azure Managed Redis before the retirement date.

microsoft.healthcareapis/workspaces/fhir.Description: Applications relying on SMART on FHIR proxy report errors in accessing the FHIR service after the retirement date.
microsoft.healthcareapis/workspaces/fhir.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.healthcareapis/workspaces/fhirservices' and properties.authenticationConfiguration.smartProxyEnabled == 'true' | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.network/networkwatchers.Description: Azure Network Watcher offers tools for monitoring, diagnosing, viewing metrics, and managing logs for IaaS resources. It helps maintain the health of VMs, virtual networks, application gateways, load balancers, but not for PaaS or Web analytics.
microsoft.network/networkwatchers.ARG_Query: resources | where location <> 'global' | summarize by location, subscriptionId | project location, subscriptionId | join kind = leftouter ( resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/networkwatchers' | summarize by location, subscriptionId | project nwlocation = location, nwsubscriptionId = subscriptionId ) on $left.location == $right.nwlocation, $left.subscriptionId == $right.nwsubscriptionId | where isnull(nwlocation) or isempty(nwlocation) | project recommendationId = '4e133bd0-8762-bc40-a95b-b29142427d73', name = strcat('NetworkWatcher_',location) , id=subscriptionId, tags='', param1 = strcat('missingLocation: ', location)

Microsoft.StreamAnalytics/streamingjobs.Description: Stream Analytics cluster (dedicated) offers more reliable performance guarantees. All the jobs running on your cluster belong only to you. You can also have access to important features like private endpoints, Auto-Scaling, Vnet Support, etc.
Microsoft.StreamAnalytics/streamingjobs.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.StreamAnalytics/streamingjobs' | where isnull(properties.cluster.id) | project recommendationId = 'e48a7227-5ec7-463a-b955-ee7cb598ded4', name, id, tags

microsoft.labservices/labaccounts.Description: After the retirement date, Azure Lab Services aren't supported. You will not have access to the lab accounts, lab plans, or labs.
microsoft.labservices/labaccounts.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.labservices/labaccounts' | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Migrate production workload on stand-alone virtual machine (VM) to multiple VMs grouped in a Virtual Machine Scale Sets Flex to intelligently distribute across the platform.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | where isnull(properties.virtualMachineScaleSet.id) | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: The maintenance configuration settings let users schedule and manage updates, making sure the updates or interruptions on the VM are performed within a planned timeframe.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | extend resourceId = tolower(id), hostId = tostring(properties.host.id), hostGroupId = tostring(properties.hostGroup.id), vmSize = tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) | where isnotempty(hostId) or isnotempty(hostGroupId) or ( vmSize contains 'i_' or vmSize contains 'is_' or vmSize contains 'id_' or vmSize contains 'ids_' or vmSize contains 'idms_' or vmSize contains 'ims_' or vmSize contains 'G5' or vmSize contains 'GS5' or vmSize contains 'F72s_' or vmSize contains 'M128ms' ) | join kind=leftouter ( resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.maintenance/configurationassignments' | extend resourceId = tolower(tostring(properties.resourceId)) | project resourceId, maintenanceProps = properties ) on resourceId | where isnull(maintenanceProps) | project recommendationId = '52ab9e5c-eec0-3148-8bd7-b6dd9e1be870', name, id, tags | order by id asc

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Use Azure Capacity Reservation for virtual machine (VM) that runs critical workloads. Azure Capacity Reservations reserve compute capacity in a specific region or availability zone.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | where isnull(properties.capacityReservation) | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Accelerated networking enables SR-IOV to a VM, greatly improving its networking performance by bypassing the host from the data path, which reduces latency, jitter, and CPU utilization for demanding network workloads on supported VM types.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | mv-expand nic = properties.networkProfile.networkInterfaces | project name, id, tags, lowerCaseNicId = tolower(nic.id), vmSize = tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) | join kind = inner ( resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces' | where properties.enableAcceleratedNetworking == false | project nicName = split(id, '/')[8], lowerCaseNicId = tolower(id) ) on lowerCaseNicId | summarize nicNames = make_set(nicName) by name, id, tostring(tags), vmSize | extend param1 = strcat('NicName: ', strcat_array(nicNames, ', ')), param2 = strcat('VMSize: ', vmSize) | project recommendationId = 'dfedbeb1-1519-fc47-86a5-52f96cf07105', name, id, tags, param1, param2 | order by id asc

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: The v5 VMs provides newer and faster cores, increased networking throughputs, and better global redundancy and availability. Upgrade to the v5 VMs from the v3 or v2 VMs to match or exceed performance at the same cost.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: Resources | where type == "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines" and location in ("australiaeast","southeastasia","swedencentral","southafricanorth","koreacentral","canadacentral","francecentral","germanywestcentral","italynorth","norwayeast","polandcentral","spaincentral","switzerlandnorth","mexicocentral","uaenorth","israelcentral","brazilsoutheast") and properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize in ( "Standard_D1_v2", "Standard_D2_v2", "Standard_D3_v2", "Standard_D4_v2", "Standard_D5_v2", "Standard_DS1_v2", "Standard_DS2_v2", "Standard_DS3_v2", "Standard_DS4_v2", "Standard_DS5_v2", "Standard_D11_v2", "Standard_D12_v2", "Standard_D13_v2", "Standard_D14_v2", "Standard_D15_v2", "Standard_DS11_v2", "Standard_DS12_v2", "Standard_DS13_v2", "Standard_DS14_v2", "Standard_DS15_v2", "Standard_D2_v3", "Standard_D4_v3", "Standard_D8_v3", "Standard_D16_v3", "Standard_D32_v3", "Standard_D48_v3", "Standard_D64_v3", "Standard_D2s_v3", "Standard_D4s_v3", "Standard_D8s_v3", "Standard_D16s_v3", "Standard_D32s_v3", "Standard_D48s_v3", "Standard_D64s_v3") | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Migrate D, Ds, Dv2, Dsv2, and Ls series VM instances to newer VM generation instances. D, Ds, Dv2, Dsv2, and Ls series VMs in Azure Virtual Machines are retiring.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources|where type == 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' and properties['hardwareProfile']['vmSize'] in ('Standard_L4s', 'Standard_L8s', 'Standard_L16s', 'Standard_L32s', 'Standard_DS1_v2', 'Standard_DS3_v2', 'Standard_DS14_v2', 'Standard_DS2_v2', 'Standard_DS13_v2', 'Standard_D1_v2', 'Standard_DS4_v2', 'Standard_D2_v2', 'Standard_DS5_v2', 'Standard_DS11_v2', 'Standard_D3_v2', 'Standard_D4_v2', 'Standard_D14_v2', 'Standard_DS12_v2', 'Standard_D11_v2', 'Standard_DS15_v2', 'Standard_D12_v2', 'Standard_D13_v2', 'Standard_DS12-2_v2', 'Standard_D5_v2', 'Standard_DS1', 'Standard_D15_v2', 'Standard_DS13-4_v2', 'Standard_D2', 'Standard_DS12-1_v2', 'Standard_DS11-1_v2', 'Standard_DS3', 'Standard_DS2', 'Standard_DS3_v2_Promo', 'Standard_DS2_v2_Promo', 'Standard_D1', 'Standard_D2_v2_Promo', 'Standard_DS14-8_v2', 'Standard_D4', 'Standard_DS13-2_v2', 'Standard_D3', 'Standard_DS5_v2_Promo', 'Standard_D4_v2_Promo', 'Standard_DS4_v2_Promo', 'Standard_DS4', 'Standard_DS14-4_v2', 'Standard_D3_v2_Promo', 'Standard_D5_v2_Promo')|project id, subscriptionId

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Move virtual machines to the same region as the related resource group. This way, Azure Resource Manager stores metadata related to all resources within the group in one region. By co-locating, you reduce the chance of being affected by region unavailability.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' | project id, vmLocation = location, subscriptionId, resourceGroup | join kind=inner ( resourcecontainers | where type == 'microsoft.resources/subscriptions/resourcegroups' | project rgLocation = location, subscriptionId, resourceGroup ) on subscriptionId, resourceGroup | where vmLocation != rgLocation | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Upgrade the operating system (OS) disk from Standard HDD to Standard SSD for increased uptime of single-instance virtual machine and improved input/output operations and throughput.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | extend provisioningState = tostring(properties.provisioningState) | extend creationTime = todatetime(properties.timeCreated) | extend osDiskSKU = tostring(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.managedDisk.storageAccountType) | extend isEphemeral = tostring(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.diffDiskSettings) | extend runningState = tostring(properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.displayStatus) | where provisioningState == "Succeeded" | where osDiskSKU == "Standard_LRS" | where isempty(isEphemeral) | where creationTime < ago(7d) and creationTime > ago(8d) | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Trusted Launch (TL) offers a modern and operational technologies for Azure virtual machines, using Secure Boot, virtual TPM, and guest attestation. This Generation 2 VM(s) have an opportunity to upgrade to Trusted Launch. Ensure this VM(s) has both an image and VM size that it is TL compatible.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type == "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines" | extend hyperV = tostring(properties['extended']['instanceView']['hyperVGeneration']) | extend security_type = tostring(properties['securityProfile']['securityType']) | where hyperV == "V2" | where security_type != "TrustedLaunch" | where security_type != "ConfidentialVM" | project id, subscriptionId

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: To avoid service disruptions, migrate workloads to the Azure NVadsA10_v5-series VMs. Azure NVadsA10_v5-series VMs include increased GPU memory bandwidth per GPU, Small AI workloads and GPU accelerated graphics applications, virtual desktops, and visualizations.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' | where tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in~ ('Standard_NV12hs_v3','Standard_NV12s_v3', 'Standard_NV24s_v3', 'Standard_NV24ms_v3', 'Standard_NV32ms_v3', 'Standard_NV48s_v3') | project id, subscriptionId

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Migrate workloads from virtual machine (VM) to Virtual Machine Scale Sets Flex for deployment across zones or within the same zone across different fault domains.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | where isnotnull(properties.availabilitySet) | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Compared to Standard HDD and SSD, Premium SSD, SSD v2, and Ultra Disks offer improved performance, configurability, and higher single-instance VM uptime SLAs. The lowest SLA of all disks on a VM applies, so it is best to use Premium or Ultra Disks for the highest uptime SLA.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | extend lname = tolower(name) | join kind=leftouter(resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/disks' | where not(sku.tier =~ 'Premium') and not(sku.tier =~ 'Ultra') | extend lname = tolower(tostring(split(managedBy, '/')[8])) | project lname, name | summarize disks = make_list(name) by lname) on lname | where isnotnull(disks) | project recommendationId = 'df0ff862-814d-45a3-95e4-4fad5a244ba6', name, id, tags, param1=strcat('AffectedDisks: ', disks)

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Virtual machines are resilient to regional outages when replication to another region is enabled. To reduce adverse business effect during an Azure region outage, the platform recommends enabling replication of all business-critical virtual machines.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | extend securityType = iif(isnull(properties.securityProfile.securityType), 'Standard', properties.securityProfile.securityType) | where securityType !in~ ('ConfidentialVM') | project id, vmIdForJoin = tolower(id), subscriptionId | join kind = leftouter (recoveryservicesresources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.RecoveryServices/vaults/replicationFabrics/replicationProtectionContainers/replicationProtectedItems' and properties.providerSpecificDetails.dataSourceInfo.datasourceType =~ 'AzureVm' | project vmResourceId = tolower(properties.providerSpecificDetails.dataSourceInfo.resourceId)) on $left.vmIdForJoin == $right.vmResourceId | where isempty(vmResourceId) | project id, subscriptionId

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Migrate production workload from A-series or B-series virtual machine (VM) to D-series or better VM. A-series and B-series VMs are designed for entry-level workloads.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' | where properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize contains 'Standard_B' or properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize contains 'Standard_A' | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: To avoid service disruptions, migrate workloads to Azure NVads_V710_v5-series virtual machines. NVads_V710_v5-series virtual machines provide greater GPU memory bandwidth per GPU for small AI workloads and GPU accelerated graphics applications, virtual desktops, and visualizations.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' | where tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in~ ('Standard_NV4as_v4','Standard_NV8as_v4','Standard_NV16as_v4','Standard_NV32as_v4','Standard_NV4ahs_v4','Standard_NV8ahs_v4','Standard_NV16ahs_v4','Standard_NV32ahs_v4') | project id,subscriptionId

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Azure Virtual Machine (VM) series F, Fs, Fsv2, Lsv2, G, Gs, Av2, and B are retiring. The VM series are no longer available for use or purchase. Applications and workloads currently operating on VM types must be migrated to newer VM series.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' | where tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) startswith 'Standard_Lsv2' or tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) startswith 'Standard_GS' or tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) startswith 'Standard_G' or tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) startswith 'Standard_Fsv2' or tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) startswith 'Standard_Fs' or tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) startswith 'Standard_F' or tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) startswith 'Standard_B' or tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) startswith 'Standard_Av2' | project id,subscriptionId

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Migrate your data from Azure unmanaged disk storage to managed disks
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' and isempty(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.managedDisk) | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Add a second instance VM to Availability Set or upgrade to Premium SSD managed disks for highest uptime.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | where isnotnull(properties.availabilitySet.id) | extend isVMpremiumCapable = iif((tostring(split(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize,'_')[1])) contains "s", 1,0) | extend osDisk = iif(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.diffDiskSettings.option == 'Local', 'Ephemeral', properties.storageProfile.osDisk.managedDisk.storageAccountType) | where tolower(osDisk) contains 'standard' and isVMpremiumCapable == 1 | project subscriptionId, id, osDisk, asid=tolower(tostring(properties.availabilitySet.id)) | join kind = inner (resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/availabilitysets' and array_length(properties.virtualMachines)==1 | project asid=tolower(tostring(id)) ) on $left.asid == $right.asid | project subscriptionId,id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: IP forwarding allows a virtual machine network interface to receive and send network traffic not destined for or originating from its assigned IP addresses.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | where isnotnull(properties.networkProfile.networkInterfaces) | mv-expand nic=properties.networkProfile.networkInterfaces | project name, id, tags, nicId = nic.id | extend nicId = tostring(nicId) | join kind=inner ( resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces' | where properties.enableIPForwarding == true | project nicId = tostring(id) ) on nicId | project recommendationId = '41a22a5e-5e08-9647-92d0-2ffe9ef1bdad', name, id, tags | order by id asc

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: After the retirement date, the Desired State Configuration Extension for Azure Virtual Machines isn't supported.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: Resources | where type == 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' | extend JoinID = toupper(id), OSName = tostring(properties.osProfile.computerName), OSType = tostring(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.osType) | join kind=inner(Resources | where type == 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines/extensions' | extend VMId = toupper(substring(id, 0, indexof(id, '/extensions'))), ExtensionName = tolower(name) | where ExtensionName == 'microsoft.powershell.dsc') on $left.JoinID == $right.VMId | project subscriptionId, id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Test recommendation for Contoso
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where subscriptionId in ('1f847e97-a9e0-4335-9e1a-531c116d57e0','d0ec37a4-206c-4f76-8410-8cdb6229974c') | where type == 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' | project id, subscriptionId, name

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: Use a Premium SSD managed disk in a Single Instance virtual machine for the highest uptime. Conversion is allowed from a Standard managed disk to a Premium managed disk.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | where isnull(properties.availabilitySet.id) and isnull(zones) and isnull(properties.host) and isnull(properties.virtualMachineScaleSet) | extend isVMpremiumCapable = iif((tostring(split(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize,'_')[1])) contains "s", 1,0) | extend osDisk = iif(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.diffDiskSettings.option == 'Local', 'Ephemeral', properties.storageProfile.osDisk.managedDisk.storageAccountType) | where tolower(osDisk) contains 'standard' and isVMpremiumCapable == 1 | project subscriptionId,id

Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.Description: When Accelerated Networking is enabled, the default Azure virtual network interface in GuestOS is swapped for a Mellanox, and its driver comes from a 3rd party. Marketplace images have the latest Mellanox drivers, but post-deployment, updating the driver is the user's responsibility.
Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | mv-expand nic = properties.networkProfile.networkInterfaces | project name, id, tags, lowerCaseNicId = tolower(nic.id), vmSize = tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) | join kind = inner ( resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces' | where properties.enableAcceleratedNetworking == true | project nicName = split(id, '/')[8], lowerCaseNicId = tolower(id) ) on lowerCaseNicId | summarize nicNames = make_set(nicName) by name, id, tostring(tags), vmSize | extend param2 = strcat('NicName: ', strcat_array(nicNames, ', ')), param3 = strcat('VMSize: ', vmSize) | project recommendationId = '73d1bb04-7d3e-0d47-bc0d-63afe773b5fe', name, id, tags, param1='This machine has AccelNet enabled, but it is not possible to confirm if the GuestOS drivers are Up-to-Date.', param2,param3 | order by id asc

microsoft.network/vpnsites.Description: Deploying active-active VPN concentrators leverages the High Availability capabilities of the VPN Gateway on Virtual Hubs using a fully-meshed topology with four IPSec tunnels.
microsoft.network/vpnsites.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.network/vpnsites' | extend vpnSiteLinks = properties.vpnSiteLinks | mv-expand vpnSiteLink = vpnSiteLinks | extend virtualWanId = tostring(properties.virtualWan.id) | summarize vpnSiteLinksCount = count() by virtualWanId | where vpnSiteLinksCount < 2 | join ( resources | where type == 'microsoft.network/virtualwans' | project id, name, tags ) on $left.virtualWanId == $right.id | project recommendationId = '02bdbdb8-d138-4090-951c-23e45b8700f7', name, id, tags, param1 = vpnSiteLinksCount

microsoft.recoveryservices/vaults/replic.Description: Perform a test failover to validate Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery strategy and ensure that the applications are functioning correctly in the target region without impacting production environment.
microsoft.recoveryservices/vaults/replic.ARG_Query: recoveryservicesresources | where type == 'microsoft.recoveryservices/vaults/replicationfabrics/replicationprotectioncontainers/replicationprotecteditems' | where properties.providerSpecificDetails.dataSourceInfo.datasourceType == 'AzureVm' and isnull(properties.lastSuccessfulTestFailoverTime) | extend id=tolower(properties.providerSpecificDetails.dataSourceInfo.resourceId) | project id, subscriptionId | join kind=inner (resources | project id = tolower(id) ) on id | project id, subscriptionId

MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKWATCHERS/FLOWLO.Description: Network security group (NSG) flow logs in Azure Network Watcher is retiring. As part of this retirement, customers will no longer be able to create new NSG flow logs. Migrate to virtual network flow logs in Network Watcher.
MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKWATCHERS/FLOWLO.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKWATCHERS/FLOWLOGS' | where properties['targetResourceId'] contains 'Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups' | project id, subscriptionId

MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKWATCHERS/FLOWLO.Description: Improves monitoring, security, and troubleshooting for Azure and Hybrid connectivity.
MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKWATCHERS/FLOWLO.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/networkwatchers/flowlogs' | where properties.targetResourceId contains 'microsoft.network/virtualNetworks' | where not(properties.flowAnalyticsConfiguration.networkWatcherFlowAnalyticsConfiguration.enabled) | project recommendationId = 'bf0b7dbd-016d-458c-af99-70fcb03ad451', name, id, tags, param1= 'Flow Analytics Configuration is disabled',param2=strcat('Vnet Name : ', properties.targetResourceId)

MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKWATCHERS/FLOWLO.Description: Network security group flow logging is a feature of Azure Network Watcher that logs IP traffic info through a network security group. If in Failed state, monitoring data from the associated resource is not collected.
MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKWATCHERS/FLOWLO.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/networkwatchers/flowlogs' and isnotnull(properties) | extend targetResourceId = tostring(properties.targetResourceId) | extend status = iff(properties.enabled =~ 'true', 'Enabled', 'Disabled') | extend provisioningState = tostring(properties.provisioningState) | extend flowLogType = iff(properties.targetResourceId contains 'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks', 'Virtual network', 'Network security group') | where provisioningState != 'Succeeded' or status != 'Enabled' | project recommendationId = '22a769ed-0ecb-8b49-bafe-8f52e6373d9c', name, id, tags, param1 = strcat('provisioningState:', provisioningState), param2=strcat('Status:', status), param3=strcat('targetResourceId:',targetResourceId), param4=strcat('flowLogType:',flowLogType)

microsoft.recoveryservices/vaults.Description: Soft delete keeps deleted backups for 14 days, so you can restore backups without losing data or paying extra. Soft delete is turned on by default and shouldn�t be turned off.
microsoft.recoveryservices/vaults.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.recoveryservices/vaults' | mv-expand issoftDelete=properties.securitySettings.softDeleteSettings.softDeleteState | where issoftDelete == 'Disabled' | project recommendationId = '9e39919b-78af-4a0b-b70f-c548dae97c25', name, id, tags, param1=strcat('Soft Delete: ',issoftDelete)

microsoft.devices/iothubs.Description: Device Provisioning Service (DPS) enables easy redistribution of IoT devices for scaling and availability, allowing devices to be reassigned and not bound to specific IoT Hub instances. Devices in IoT Hubs using DPS should be verified for DPS utilization.
microsoft.devices/iothubs.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.devices/iothubs' | project id, iotHubName=tostring(properties.hostName), tags, resourceGroup | join kind=fullouter ( resources | where type == 'microsoft.devices/provisioningservices' | mv-expand iotHubs=properties.iotHubs | project iotHubName = tostring(iotHubs.name), dpsName = name, name=iotHubs.name ) on iotHubName | where dpsName == '' | project recommendationId='b1e1378d-4572-4414-bebd-b8872a6d4d1c', name=iotHubName, id, tags, param1='DPS:none'

microsoft.devices/iothubs.Description: In a production scenario, the IoT Hub tier should not be Free because the Free tier does not provide the necessary Service Level Agreement.
microsoft.devices/iothubs.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.devices/iothubs' and tostring(sku.tier) =~ 'Free' | project recommendationId='eeba3a49-fef0-481f-a471-7ff01139b474', name, id, tags, param1=strcat('tier:', tostring(sku.tier))

microsoft.devices/iothubs.Description: Using message routing for custom endpoints in IoT Hub, messages might not reach these destinations if specific conditions are unmet. A default route ensures all messages are received, but disabling this safety net risks leaving some messages undelivered.
microsoft.devices/iothubs.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.devices/iothubs' | extend fallbackEnabled=properties.routing.fallbackRoute.isEnabled | where fallbackEnabled == false | project recommendationId='e7dbd21f-b27a-4b8c-a901-cedb1e6d8e1e', name, id, tags, param1='FallbackRouteEnabled:false'

microsoft.sql/servers.Description: Support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1 on Azure SQL db is retiring. Update the TLS policy to the latest version
microsoft.sql/servers.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.sql/servers' and properties['minimalTlsVersion'] in~ ('None','1.0', '1.1') | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.eventhub/namespaces.Description: Enable auto-inflate on Event Hubs Standard tier namespaces to automatically scale up throughput units (TUs), meeting usage needs and preventing data ingress or egress throttle scenarios by adjusting to allowed rates.
microsoft.eventhub/namespaces.ARG_Query: Resources | where type == "microsoft.eventhub/namespaces" | where sku.tier == "Standard" | where isnull(properties.geoDataReplication.locations[0].clusterArmId) and isnull(properties.clusterArmId) | where properties.isAutoInflateEnabled == "false" | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.eventhub/namespaces.Description: Avoid using explicit key versions for Key Vault used for customer-managed keys in Event Hubs namespaces to enable seamless key rotation, reduce operational overhead, and prevent outages caused by expired or deleted key versions.
microsoft.eventhub/namespaces.ARG_Query: resources | where type == 'microsoft.eventhub/namespaces' | mv-expand keyVault = properties.encryption.keyVaultProperties | where isnotempty(keyVault.keyVersion) | distinct id, subscriptionId | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.eventhub/namespaces.Description: Premium Event Hub namespaces using more than 6 processing units may benefit from migrating to a dedicated cluster, which is optimized for mission-critical workloads and offers higher performance, lower latency, and flexible scaling.
microsoft.eventhub/namespaces.ARG_Query: Resources | where type == "microsoft.eventhub/namespaces" | where sku.tier == "Premium" | where sku.capacity > 6 | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.eventhub/namespaces.Description: Set up Geo-replication on Event Hubs namespaces with Premium or Dedicated SKUs to ensure high availability and regional failover. This new feature replicates both metadata and data, helping protect against outages and disasters for mission-critical workloads.
microsoft.eventhub/namespaces.ARG_Query: Resources | where type == "microsoft.eventhub/namespaces" | where isnotnull(properties.geoDataReplication.locations[0].clusterArmId) or isnotnull(properties.clusterArmId) or sku.tier == "Premium" | where array_length(properties.geoDataReplication.locations) == 1 | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.Description: Workloads running Standard_M192idms_v2 are deleted and associated application data is lost.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachine' and (tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in ('Standard_M192idms_v2') or tostring(sku.name) in ('Standard_M192idms_v2')) | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.Description: To avoid any disruption to your service, we recommend that you change the VM sizing for your workloads from the current Standard_NC24rs_v3 to the newer VM series in the same NC product line.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachine' and (tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in ('Standard_NC24rs_v3') or tostring(sku.name) in ('Standard_NC24rs_v3')) | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.Description: Standard_NC24rs_v3 virtual machine size in NCv3-series virtual machines is retiring. Upgrade to the newer VM series in the same NC product line.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachine' and (tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in~ ('Standard_NC24s_v3') or tostring(sku.name) in~ ('Standard_NC24s_v3')) | project id, subscriptionId

microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.Description: Workloads running Standard_M192is_v2 are deleted and associated application data is lost.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachine' and (tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in~ ('Standard_M192is_v2') or tostring(sku.name) in ('Standard_M192is_v2')) | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.Description: To avoid any disruption to your service, we recommend that you change the VM sizing for your workloads from the current NCv3-series VMs to the newer VM series in the same NC product line.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachine' and (tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in ('Standard_NC6s_v3','Standard_NC12s_v3','Standard_NC24s_v3') or tostring(sku.name) in ('Standard_NC6s_v3','Standard_NC12s_v3','Standard_NC24s_v3')) | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.Description: Workloads running Standard_M192ims_v2 are deleted and associated application data is lost.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachine' and (tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in ('Standard_M192ims_v2') or tostring(sku.name) in ('Standard_M192ims_v2')) | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.Description: Workloads running Standard_M192ids_v2 are deleted and associated application data is lost.
microsoft.compute/virtualmachine.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachine' and (tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize) in ('Standard_M192ids_v2') or tostring(sku.name) in ('Standard_M192ids_v2')) | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.eventgrid/namespaces.Description: Support for TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 ends on the retirement date.
microsoft.eventgrid/namespaces.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.eventgrid/namespaces' and tostring(properties['minimumTlsVersionAllowed']) in ('1.1','1.0') | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.eventgrid/partnernamespaces.Description: Support for TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 ends on the retirement date.
microsoft.eventgrid/partnernamespaces.ARG_Query: resources | where type contains 'microsoft.eventgrid/partnernamespaces' and tostring(properties['minimumTlsVersionAllowed']) in ('1.1','1.0') | project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.synapse/workspaces/bigdatapool.Description: Azure Synapse runtime for Apache Spark 3.3 are retired and disabled.
microsoft.synapse/workspaces/bigdatapool.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.synapse/workspaces/bigdatapools' and todouble(properties.sparkVersion) == 3.3 | extend workspaceId = tostring(split(id,'/')[8]) | join (resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.synapse/workspaces' and properties.adlaResourceId == '' | project workspaceId = name) on workspaceId| project subscriptionId, id

microsoft.virtualmachineimages/imagetemp.Description: The Azure Image Builder service lacks availability zones support. Replicating Image Templates to a secondary region will enable the build of new images in secondary region.
microsoft.virtualmachineimages/imagetemp.ARG_Query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.virtualmachineimages/imagetemplates' | mv-expand distribution=properties.distribute | where array_length(parse_json(distribution).replicationRegions) == 1 | project recommendationId = '21fb841b-ba70-1f4e-a460-1f72fb41aa51', name, id, param1=strcat('replicationRegions:',parse_json(distribution).replicationRegions)

microsoft.web/kubeenvironments.Description: Azure App Service on Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes is retiring. Installation of the Application Services extension is no longer available.
